Course Title: AI in Project Scheduling: When Optimisation Fails
Category: Infrastructure & Construction: Construction, Engineering, Building Materials, Real Estate Development
Who is this for: Project managers, construction managers, site supervisors, engineering project coordinators, infrastructure project leads, construction schedulers, project planners
Team or Dept this is for: Operations, Engineering, Construction, Project Management, Infrastructure Development
Description: Over-reliance on AI timelines that ignore real-world variability.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with construction or engineering project workflows and a general awareness of how scheduling tools (such as Gantt charts or project management software) are used in practice.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key limitations of AI-generated project schedules in construction and engineering contexts, and explain why over-reliance on automated timelines can introduce significant risk into real-world infrastructure projects. You will also apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI scheduling outputs, recognising where human judgement and site-level variability must be factored in to produce more reliable and resilient project programmes.
